reviews.title: Pretty good, take care of it and it’ll take care of you
reviews.body: I’m a professional detailer for a collision repair shop, I clean cars after they’ve been fixed. We use these a good bit on the cars we do when they’re done being washed, they’re great. Just make sure you wash them separately from other towels that might have chemicals or debris on them, kinda ruins the way it absorbs water. That’s the same that can be said for any towel tho, so if you have rags/towels specific to drying your car you should be washing them separately anyways. Will not scratch your car, pretty simple to use, size makes it easier to dry cars quickly, especially if you’re a smaller person and would typically have to reach across the hood or roof to get it fully dried. Pretty much the standard as far what the texture of a towel should be for using on a car if you’re serious about taking care of your paint, tons of other “microfiber” towels say they won’t scratch and do. I’ve used tons of different kinds of towels and rags, these are by far the best to use for drying. Definitely make sure you clean the car thoroughly tho because you do not want hard objects like rock or dirt to stick to the towel because you will scratch your paint, that will happen with anything you use so just be careful and mindful. Overall, 10/10. If they weren’t as expensive as they are, I’d probably have about a dozen of them for work and a couple for myself

The appeard to be extra where after sewing it was ment to be cut but wasn't not a major deal but I wonder how that will effect the long-term of the product and if after some washes if it'll fall apart.TLDR: product works great but some quality control issues.

reviews.title: Overall, highly recommended if you want a towel that actually works.
reviews.body: The Blackline 24”x36” twisted-loop drying towel is a really good product. It dries fast, leaves no streaks, and doesn’t leave lint behind. The microfiber feels high quality and it’s reusable, so it lasts a long time. One pass is all you need to dry your car clean.

reviews.title: Heavy, Thirsty Towels!
reviews.body: My husband asked me to order one for each of our vehicles and I must say I am pleased with the quality of this towel. As directed, we washed first with the suggested Blackline detergent. The towels left no residue or streaks and our vehicles are as shiny as the day we purchased. I am hoping the towels hold their own because my husband has burned a considerable amount of money over the years with other vendors. So far, we are happy with this purchase.

reviews.title: 5 Stars – Massive, Absorbent, and Exactly as Advertised
reviews.body: LOVE this towel. The Blackline 24” x 36” Twisted-Loop Car Drying Towel is absolutely as described. It’s large, super absorbent, and made drying our ’25 Escalade Sport Platinum incredibly easy. It handled the size of the vehicle with ease and made quick work of the job without feeling saturated too fast. If you want a quality drying towel that performs like it should, this one delivers. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: High quality towel for cars
reviews.body: Does a great job, no streaks!

reviews.title: Great drying towel
reviews.body: Got this for my husband for Christmas. He absolutely loves it for his car. He says it's the best drying towel he's had. Works great and leaves no streaks . Worth the price and great quality.

reviews.title: Great quality!!
reviews.body: Awesome !! In the past I would need several drying towels or my leaf blower to dry my full size pickup…This Blackline towel drys my entire truck…with NO wringing !!!Great product and highly recommend!!

reviews.title: Best drying towel on the market, hands down.
reviews.body: Well worth the price.  If you want a great quality drying towel for your car, rv, boat then this is your best bet.  My husband washed our boat and normally he will wax it too since it was in salt water, but this time he was so. pleased with how it looked after drying he skipped the waxing part.  You will not have streaks or spots after using the drying towel.

reviews.title: Pretty good, take care of it and it’ll take care of you
reviews.body: I’m a professional detailer for a collision repair shop, I clean cars after they’ve been fixed. We use these a good bit on the cars we do when they’re done being washed, they’re great. Just make sure you wash them separately from other towels that might have chemicals or debris on them, kinda ruins the way it absorbs water. That’s the same that can be said for any towel tho, so if you have rags/towels specific to drying your car you should be washing them separately anyways. Will not scratch your car, pretty simple to use, size makes it easier to dry cars quickly, especially if you’re a smaller person and would typically have to reach across the hood or roof to get it fully dried. Pretty much the standard as far what the texture of a towel should be for using on a car if you’re serious about taking care of your paint, tons of other “microfiber” towels say they won’t scratch and do. I’ve used tons of different kinds of towels and rags, these are by far the best to use for drying. Definitely make sure you clean the car thoroughly tho because you do not want hard objects like rock or dirt to stick to the towel because you will scratch your paint, that will happen with anything you use so just be careful and mindful. Overall, 10/10. If they weren’t as expensive as they are, I’d probably have about a dozen of them for work and a couple for myself

reviews.title: Pretty good, take care of it and it’ll take care of you
reviews.body: I’m a professional detailer for a collision repair shop, I clean cars after they’ve been fixed. We use these a good bit on the cars we do when they’re done being washed, they’re great. Just make sure you wash them separately from other towels that might have chemicals or debris on them, kinda ruins the way it absorbs water. That’s the same that can be said for any towel tho, so if you have rags/towels specific to drying your car you should be washing them separately anyways. Will not scratch your car, pretty simple to use, size makes it easier to dry cars quickly, especially if you’re a smaller person and would typically have to reach across the hood or roof to get it fully dried. Pretty much the standard as far what the texture of a towel should be for using on a car if you’re serious about taking care of your paint, tons of other “microfiber” towels say they won’t scratch and do. I’ve used tons of different kinds of towels and rags, these are by far the best to use for drying. Definitely make sure you clean the car thoroughly tho because you do not want hard objects like rock or dirt to stick to the towel because you will scratch your paint, that will happen with anything you use so just be careful and mindful. Overall, 10/10. If they weren’t as expensive as they are, I’d probably have about a dozen of them for work and a couple for myself
